title: First-principles framework for exchange interactions and red-shifted luminescence of ${\mathrm{Cr}}^{3+}$ ion pairs in strong crystal field environments
abstract:
Author(s): Qinshi Hu, Lingkun Zhang, Anfei Chen, Qianshan Quan, Min Yin, Chang-Kui Duan, and Qiaoling Chen
Here, the authors establish a first-principles framework, benchmarked against ruby (Al2O3:Cr3+) and applied to multiple oxides, for quantitatively determining the ground-state exchange coupling J and emission redshift of Cr3+ ion pairs in strong crystal-field environments. Redshifts increase along corner, edge, and face-sharing from less than 0.1 to ∼0.2 eV. The study shows that excited-state electron-phonon coupling and magnetic exchange work synergistically, and these geometry-specific redshift ranges can distinguish genuine pair-related near-infrared centers from other defects.
[Phys. Rev. B 113, 195156] Published Fri May 29, 2026
relevance_reason: First-principles framework for exchange and luminescence using DFT; explicit atoms and quantum chemistry calculations; meets (a)-(c); score 0.75.
